items.trigger: Primary: 15-min candle close above SMA(20) at $180.59 with volume >= 1.0x 30-day average confirming the reclaim. Alternate: intraday bounce from $176.00-$177.50 support zone with RSI(14) rising above 50 on 15-min timeframe and volume >= 0.90x average. Do not chase opens above $182.00 (extended from SMA, unfavorable R/R).

stop.rationale: Structural support just below the thesis invalidation condition of $172.00 (3.7% below current price). A close below $171.50 on above-average volume confirms China enforcement action risk is embedding in price action or institutional selling is accelerating beyond market-regime correction. 2-point buffer below the $172.00 round-number invalidation to avoid noise-driven exits.

target.rationale: Approximately 3.5% above current price, representing SMA(20) reclaim plus extension toward prior consolidation zone. Consistent with Bollinger band expansion from current %B 0.363 (lower half) targeting midline reversion. Offers ~3.2:1 R/R from SMA(20) breakout entry (~$180.59 entry) to $171.50 stop.

items.note: REDUCE_ONLY regime active: position sized at QUARTER — captures limited upside even if thesis fully plays out. Beta 2.375 means a 1% broad market decline = ~2.4% NVDA decline; stop at $171.50 can be reached on a normal market-down day. China enforcement action (March 26 headline) is a live, unresolved risk: the primary working assumption is that Supermicro is the target, not NVDA directly — this assumption has a short shelf life and can be invalidated by a follow-on report. Manufacturing restart/purchase order commentary is based on a public CEO statement (March 23); not confirmed via 8-K or SEC filing. Five insider Form 4 filings (direction unconfirmed) represent an unresolvable gap — if they are confirmed sales, the insider signal is adverse. Earnings May 20 (55 days) is outside the hold window but any pre-announcement risk should be monitored.

items.trigger: Primary: 30-min candle close above $1,395 on first-hour consolidation with volume >= 0.95x average, confirming SK Hynix catalyst continuation. Alternate: intraday pullback to SMA(20) zone ($1,376-$1,382) with a confirmed 15-min bounce candle and volume >= 1.0x average (structural support hold). Do not enter if price is more than 2% above $1,395 at time of evaluation (gap-chasing risk into DCF overvaluation).

stop.rationale: Two points below SMA(20) at ~$1,377-$1,378. A close below $1,375 on above-average volume confirms the thesis invalidation condition (SMA(20) breakdown = institutional de-risking pre-earnings). Buffer accounts for intraday noise. ATR% 4.14% means a 1-standard-deviation intraday move can reach this level; stop is intentionally tight given QUARTER sizing — position is small enough to absorb if triggered.

target.rationale: Approximately 4% above current price ($1,393.89), representing SK Hynix order catalyst extension toward prior range top. Offers approximately 2.8:1 R/R from current price to $1,375 stop (~$19 risk, ~$56 gain). Consistent with upper Bollinger band expansion from current %B 0.605.

items.note: REDUCE_ONLY regime active: position sized at QUARTER. Earnings April 15 (20 days) is the single largest fragility — prior quarter missed by -2.72% against $6.65 estimate; this represents a binary event risk that can fully reverse any premarket catalyst gains. Hard rule: exit position before April 15 regardless of target status. DCF model estimates stock 77% above intrinsic value — the current price requires the SK Hynix order and sustained EUV demand acceleration to be structurally real, not pulled-forward. Retail bearish sentiment diverges from institutional (institutional bullish) with reversing momentum — retail may be correctly positioned against the recent institutional move. ATR% 4.14% makes the stop expensive even at QUARTER sizing. Assumes SK Hynix order is firm-committed — if this assumption breaks, the entire catalyst disappears and stop may not protect from a gap down.

items.trigger: Primary: 30-min candle close above $349.50 (confirmed breakout above SMA(20) at ~$348.00) with volume >= 0.90x average (accounting for slight below-average trend). Alternate: intraday dip to $344-$346 structural support with 15-min bounce candle confirming RSI(14) turning up from below 45, volume >= 0.85x average. Do not enter on a gap-up open above $352 without subsequent consolidation and retest.

stop.rationale: Structural support approximately 2.3% below SMA(20) (~$348.00). A close below $340 on above-average volume confirms institutional de-risking ahead of April 16 earnings, matching the thesis invalidation condition. Round number support with sufficient buffer above SMA(20) breakdown zone to avoid noise triggers while being actionable.

target.rationale: Approximately 3% above current price ($347.75), targeting upper Bollinger band expansion from current %B 0.495 midline. Represents a clean RSI recovery toward 52-55 range confirming continued institutional accumulation on SK Hynix/ASML foundry read-through. Offers ~2.2:1 R/R from SMA(20) breakout entry (~$349.50) to $340 stop.

items.note: REDUCE_ONLY regime active: position sized at QUARTER. Short-term MA buy signal vs long-term MA sell signal is a genuine technical divergence — the 7-day hold operates within the short-term timeframe but the longer trend provides a headwind on recovery. Earnings April 16 (21 days) with elevated expectations ($3.30 vs $3.14 prior actual) — the execution bar is higher than both prior beats; a miss or soft guidance would sharply reprice the stock. Retail bullish divergence from institutional neutral (-7pt adjustment) suggests retail may be positioned ahead of institutional confirmation; this is a contrarian risk if institutions de-risk pre-earnings. Geopolitical Taiwan risk is permanently latent and immune to modeling — any escalation would override all stop and target levels. Volume ratio 0.86 (slightly below average) means the current outperformance lacks strong confirmation; the SK Hynix read-through is an inference, not a direct TSM announcement.

items.trigger: Primary: 15-min candle close above $77.00 (clearing premarket hesitation zone) with volume >= 0.85x average and RSI(14) rising from current 51 toward 53+. Confirms BULLISH technical continuation with Wolfe/BofA analyst momentum. Alternate: intraday pullback to SMA(20) zone ($75.00-$75.75) with a confirmed 15-min bounce candle and volume >= 0.90x average (structural support hold). Do not enter on gap-up open above $79.00 without consolidation.

stop.rationale: One point below SMA(20) at ~$75.50. A close below $74.50 represents a full SMA breakdown signaling institutional de-risking and thesis invalidation. Risk from current price: ~$2.11/share (~2.75%), which is manageable at QUARTER sizing within RL-002 (0.35% account equity per trade). Sufficient buffer to avoid noise triggers while providing a clean structural break signal.

target.rationale: Approximately 7% above current price, representing partial recovery toward the Wolfe Research $96 target backed by the $9.9B FCF 2026 model. Prior resistance zone consistent with MEDIUM hold and catalyst-driven momentum (Wolfe $96 + BofA upgrade + autonomous driving launch). Offers approximately 3.5:1 R/R from $77.00 breakout entry to $74.50 stop.

items.note: REDUCE_ONLY regime active: position sized at QUARTER. Despite being the highest bull_pct candidate (58%) with ADD sentiment (corroborated retail + institutional alignment), REDUCE_ONLY regime constraint is binding on sizing. Q1 EPS miss of -305.52% (actual -$3.60 vs +$1.75 estimate) only 2 months ago (Cruise wind-down charges) — while classified as non-recurring, any Cruise-related follow-on write-down or charge would severely undercut the bull case. Profit margin 1.46% means operational leverage cuts both ways — tariff changes, input cost inflation, or autonomous program costs can eliminate earnings entirely. Wolfe Research $96 FCF model is based on truck refresh and US production shift assumptions that are forward-looking and unconfirmed in actual filings. Autonomous road test launch is supervised-only with safety drivers — no revenue timeline; the narrative catalyst is real but the monetization timeline is speculative. Multiple insider Form 4 filings in March (direction unconfirmed) represent an unresolvable gap for this cycle. Volume ratio 0.88 (near average) confirms the move is not yet institutionally driven at scale.

hedges.routing_reason: VIX=29.75 (HIGH) but HMM=CALM (vol_prob=0%) — both BPS + protective puts
